title: ConfigValue v1.0
description: A single dated ConfigValue record. Mirrors the Pydantic model in src/govops/config.py. Per ADR-006, granularity is per-parameter (one record per leaf value). Per ADR-003, on-disk YAML omits id and created_at — those are assigned by the loader.

id.description: Stable identifier. ULID format when generated by the loader; omitted on-disk.

domain.description: Top-level grouping. The vocabulary is open but the canonical set is fixed during Phases 1-4.

key.description: Full dotted key, e.g. 'ca.rule.age-65.min_age'. Resolution is by (key, evaluation_date, jurisdiction_id).

jurisdiction_id.description: Jurisdiction scope. null or 'global' means cross-jurisdictional.

value.description: The resolved value. Shape must match value_type.

value_type.description: Discriminator on the shape of `value`. The loader uses this for round-trip type fidelity.

effective_from.description: ISO-8601 date or datetime. Records with no effective_to remain in effect indefinitely.

effective_to.description: ISO-8601 date or datetime. Exclusive upper bound (evaluation_date < effective_to).

citation.description: Statutory or doctrinal citation backing this value (free-form).

author.description: Who wrote this record. Defaults to 'system:yaml' for loader-inserted records.

approved_by.description: Per ADR-008, prompt records require this field for dual approval. Other domains may leave it null.

rationale.description: Why this value, in human terms. Empty string is allowed but discouraged.

supersedes.description: id of the prior version this record replaces. Forms a linked-list version chain.

status.description: Approval state. Only 'approved' records participate in resolve().

language.description: BCP-47 language tag. Used by ui.label records to scope per-language values.

allOf.description: Per ADR-008 Gate 4, prompt records carry both author and approved_by.
Per ADR-012, template records require dual approval (same operating shape as prompts; reviewer competence pool differs).
